Package com.salesforce.multicloudj.registry.driver

Class AbstractRegistry

java.lang.Object
com.salesforce.multicloudj.registry.driver.AbstractRegistry
All Implemented Interfaces:
Provider, AuthProvider, AutoCloseable
Direct Known Subclasses:
AwsRegistry, GcpRegistry
public abstract class AbstractRegistry extends Object implements Provider, AutoCloseable, AuthProvider
Abstract registry driver. Each cloud implements authentication and OCI client.

    • getOciClient

      Returns the OCI client for this registry.

    • getInterceptors

      protected List<org.apache.http.HttpRequestInterceptor> getInterceptors()
      Returns the list of HTTP request interceptors to be registered with the HTTP client. Override this method in provider-specific subclasses to add custom interceptors. By default, returns an empty list (no interceptors).

    • pull

      public Image pull(String imageRef)
      Pulls an image from the registry (unified OCI flow).

      This method implements the OCI image pull workflow:

      1. Parse the image reference to extract repository and reference (tag or digest)
      2. Fetch the manifest from the registry
      3. If the manifest is a multi-arch index, select a platform-specific manifest
      4. Return a RemoteImage that lazily loads layers on demand

      The returned Image uses lazy loading - blobs are only downloaded when accessed via Image.getLayers().

      Parameters:
      imageRef - image reference (e.g. "my-repo/my-image:latest" or "my-repo/my-image@sha256:...")
      Returns:
      Image metadata and layer descriptors (lazy-loaded)
      Throws:
      InvalidArgumentException - if the image reference format is invalid
      UnknownException - if the pull fails (network error, authentication failure, manifest not found, etc.)

    • selectPlatformFromIndex

      protected String selectPlatformFromIndex(Manifest indexManifest, Platform platform)
      Selects a platform-specific manifest from a multi-arch image index.

      This method matches the target platform against each entry in the index using the Platform.matches() method. The first matching entry is selected.

      Platform matching follows OCI specification:

      • OS, Architecture, Variant, and OS version must match
      • OS features in the spec must be a subset of the entry's OS features
      • Empty/null fields in the target platform are treated as wildcards
      Parameters:
      indexManifest - the image index manifest (must be an index)
      platform - the target platform to match against
      Returns:
      the digest of the selected platform-specific manifest
      Throws:
      UnknownException - if no matching platform is found in the index
      InvalidArgumentException - if the manifest is not an index

    • extract

      public InputStream extract(Image image)
      Extracts the image filesystem as a tar stream.

      This method implements OCI layer flattening.

      1. Layers are processed in order (bottom to top)
      2. Each layer is a tar archive that is decompressed and streamed
      3. Whiteout files (.wh.*) mark deletions from lower layers
      4. Opaque whiteouts (.wh..wh..opq) indicate directory replacement

      The returned InputStream produces a tar archive representing the flattened filesystem. Caller is responsible for closing the stream.

      Parameters:
      image - image from a previous pull
      Returns:
      InputStream of the flattened filesystem tar
      Throws:
      InvalidArgumentException - if image is null or has no layers
      UnknownException - if extraction fails

    • getException

      public abstract Class<? extends SubstrateSdkException> getException(Throwable t)
      Description copied from interface: Provider
      Maps a given Throwable from the provider implementation to a specific SubstrateSdkException. This is used for exception handling abstraction.
      Specified by:
      getException in interface Provider
      Parameters:
      t - The Throwable to be mapped.
      Returns:
      The Class of the corresponding SubstrateSdkException.
